generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Heterogeneous IoT/industrial sensor measuring temperature parameters for contextual data aggregation in the Digital Shadow Reference Model."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance PressureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Industrial sensor capturing pressure measurements for real-time or near-real-time data collection in manufacturing systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PressureDataTransmitted
]

instance VibrationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor detecting vibration patterns for predictive maintenance and anomaly detection in physical systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VibrationDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring environmental parameters (e.g., humidity, ambient temperature) for contextual data traces in the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance PressureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PressureSensor
]

instance VibrationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VibrationSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalSensor
]

instance OperationalMetricsDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor, PressureSensor, VibrationSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ContextualDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated and abstracted sensor data traces filtered for specific purposes (e.g., monitoring, analytics) as defined in the Digital Shadow Reference Model."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, PressureDataTransmitted, VibrationDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance HistoricalDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data traces used for forecasting and predictive maintenance, combining historical patterns with behavioral models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalMetricsDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimeSeriesAnalysisEnabler
]

instance SysMLBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "State machine model (SysML v1.7) capturing functional behavior of components for anomaly detection and behavioral validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnabler, BehavioralValidationEnabler
]

instance MontiArcAutomaton : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-based automaton model for behavioral control logic in the Digital Twin, integrated with MontiArc framework."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlAlgorithmEnabler
]

instance SimulinkSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation model in MathWorks Simulink for detailed behavioral validation and performance optimization of system dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnabler, OptimizationEnabler
]

instance AADLComponentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Architecture Description Language (AADL) model defining component-connector perspectives for structural validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StructuralValidationEnabler
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML IBD model representing component-connector compositions for hierarchical system decomposition."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MBSEIntegrationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ETL pipeline for preprocessing and filtering raw sensor data into purpose-driven contextual data traces."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance TimeSeriesAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series analysis framework for forecasting system behavior and predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService, ForecastingService
]

instance SimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink integration for detailed behavioral simulation and system dynamics validation."
    DTDFVocab:enables PerformanceOptimizationService
]

instance BehavioralValidationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML v1.7 state machine validation framework for behavioral model verification."
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance StructuralValidationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AADL-based structural validation tool for component-connector architecture validation."
    DTDFVocab:enables SystemDesignService
]

instance ControlAlgorithmEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "C++/Java-based implementation of closed-loop control algorithms for autonomous decision support."
    DTDFVocab:enables ClosedLoopControlService
]

instance MBSEIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware framework for integrating SysML models with simulation tools and databases."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance OptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ADL-based logical architecture optimization tool for resource allocation and workflow efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:enables PerformanceOptimizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time state tracking service providing continuous aggregation of sensor data traces for operational technicians."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeStateTrackingInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Behavioral model-based anomaly detection service identifying deviations from expected operational patterns."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight, MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance service combining historical data traces with behavioral models to forecast equipment failures."
    DTDFVocab:provides FailureForecastInsight, InterventionSchedulingAction
]

instance ForecastingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Forecasting service applying time-series analysis to predict system behavior under varying conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemBehaviorForecastInsight
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Performance tuning service using simulation tools to optimize system parameters for resource allocation."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationInsight
]

instance ClosedLoopControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop feedback service enabling active data processing to adjust physical system parameters via control algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAdjustmentAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D representation service rendering multi-layered system models for intuitive understanding."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemVisualizationInsight
]

instance SystemDesignService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "System design service providing structural validation and component-connector architecture insights."
    DTDFVocab:provides DesignValidationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeStateTrackingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous aggregation of sensor data traces for context-aware monitoring of physical system states."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of deviations from expected operational patterns using behavioral models (state machines, automata)."
]

instance FailureForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecast of equipment failures based on historical data traces and behavioral model analysis."
]

instance SystemBehaviorForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Prediction of system behavior under varying conditions using time-series analysis and simulation engines."
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommendations for system parameter optimization derived from simulation-based analysis."
]

instance SystemVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D visualization of multi-layered system models for stakeholder understanding."
]

instance DesignValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation insights for component-connector architectures using ADL-based structural validation."
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated recommendation for maintenance interventions based on anomaly detection and failure forecasting."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance InterventionSchedulingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated scheduling of maintenance interventions based on predictive maintenance forecasts."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Autonomous adjustment of physical system parameters via closed-loop control algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance ManufacturingSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains TemperatureSensor, PressureSensor, VibrationSensor, EnvironmentalSensor,
                 ContextualDataTrace, HistoricalDataTrace,
                 SysMLBehavioralModel, MontiArcAutomaton,
                 SimulinkSimulationModel, AADLComponentModel,
                 SysMLInternalBlockDiagram
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system leverages **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which require precise temporal synchronization between physical and digital components across multiple abstraction layers (e.g., requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers). Synchronization frequencies are implicitly tied to the **architecture description languages (ADLs)** and **simulation tools** (e.g., SysML, MontiArc, MathWorks Simulink) used for behavior modeling. For example, **SysML state machines** and **MontiArc Automaton** enforce discrete-time event-driven synchronization, while **Simulink** supports configurable time-step resolutions (e.g., fixed-step or variable-step solvers) for real-time or near-real-time twin updates. Latency requirements are not explicitly quantified in the document but are implied to depend on the **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** and **data aggregation needs** of digital shadows, which necessitate low-latency processing for active feedback loops. Temporal granularity is dictated by the **purpose-driven aggregation** of contextual data traces, where finer granularity (e.g., millisecond-level updates) may be required for critical operational services, while coarser granularity (e.g., second-level) suffices for monitoring or analytics. The document does not specify exact frequencies but emphasizes the need for **real-time or near-real-time synchronization** to maintain consistency between physical and digital representations."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s orchestration follows a **multi-layered, tool-integrated architecture** rooted in MBSE practices. Key components include:
   - **Modeling Tools**: SysML (for architecture), MontiArc (for automaton-based behavior), and Simulink (for dynamic simulation) are integrated to form a **heterogeneous constellation** where each tool serves a specific abstraction layer.
   - **Architecture Patterns**: ADLs like **ACME, AADL, and SysML IBDs** define **component-connector frameworks**, enabling modularity and reusability. The **MontiArc Automaton** and **SysML state machines** introduce **behavioral coordination** via state transitions and event-driven logic.
   - **Resource Management**: Implicitly handled through **tool-specific resource allocation** (e.g., Simulink’s solver configurations) and **MBSE’s layered complexity management** (e.g., RFLP’s separation of concerns). The document does not specify explicit orchestration frameworks (e.g., Kubernetes, microservices) but emphasizes **active components** (e.g., software interpreters) to process data flows, suggesting a **service-oriented constellation** where twins act as **autonomous yet coordinated entities**.
   - **System-Wide Coordination**: Achieved through **shared data models** (e.g., SysML diagrams) and **interoperability standards** (implied by the mention of heterogeneous stakeholders). The **digital shadow reference model** (Fig. 1) underscores the need for **contextual data aggregation**, which may require **centralized metadata management** to unify disparate twin instances."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. These stages are governed by methodologies such as **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM**, **SPES**, and **SMArDT**, which systematically decompose system complexity. Transition management between phases involves **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, and SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define components, interfaces, connectors, and composition hierarchies. Behavioral modeling is integrated via **state machines in SysML v1.7** or **MontiArc Automaton**, while detailed simulations are executed using tools like **MathWorks Simulink** or other simulation frameworks. Lifecycle governance ensures integration of **heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives** and **multi-tool interoperability**, addressing challenges in model consistency and traceability across development phases. The lifecycle also includes **version control** for evolving models, though explicit versioning strategies are not detailed in the provided documents.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s **fidelity and validity** are addressed through:
  - **Model fidelity requirements**: Derived from **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., **RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, SMArDT**) to ensure hierarchical abstraction alignment across **requirements, functions, logic, and physics** layers,
  - **Validation and verification methods**: Implicitly supported by **ADLs (e.g., AADL, MontiArc)** for structural correctness and **simulation tools (e.g., Simulink)** for behavioral validation,
  - **Uncertainty sources**: Quantified via **data aggregation and abstraction** in the Digital Shadow model, where **contextual traces** may introduce noise or gaps,
  - **Error detection and correction**: Not explicitly detailed, but inferred to rely on **active components** within the Digital Twin (e.g., **software interpreters**) to process and validate incoming data,
  - **Quality assurance processes**: Enforced through **interoperability frameworks** to integrate heterogeneous models/tools, ensuring consistency across **multiple stakeholders’ perspectives**."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for Digital Twin systems is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents. However, the following inferred technical aspects are implied based on broader context:
  - **Communication Protocols**: While not specified, the document references **OPC UA** (as part of data acquisition) and **MQTT** (for IoT data flows), suggesting these protocols may also govern **virtual-to-physical control interactions** in bidirectional DT systems.
  - **Network Topology/Architecture**: The document does not specify topology (e.g., star, mesh, or hierarchical), but the mention of **heterogeneous stakeholders and perspectives** implies a **distributed or hybrid architecture** (e.g., cloud-edge-on-premise) to support scalability and latency-sensitive applications.
  - **Bandwidth/Latency Requirements**: No quantitative requirements are provided, but the need for **real-time or near-real-time synchronization** (implied in physical-to-virtual interaction) suggests **low-latency networks** (e.g., <100ms for critical control loops) and **high-bandwidth connections** for large-scale data transmission.
  - **Reliability/Redundancy Mechanisms**: The document does not detail redundancy, but the reference to **active components** in DTs (vs. passive digital shadows) implies mechanisms like **failover protocols, load balancing, or duplicate data streams** to ensure uninterrupted operation.
  - **Security Protocols**: Not explicitly mentioned, but given the context of **heterogeneous integration**, protocols like **TLS/SSL, OAuth 2.0, or role-based access control (RBAC)** are likely employed to secure communication channels.

  The document does not provide specific details on **command execution confirmation** or **conflict resolution**, as these are typically addressed in **control system architectures** (e.g., using **state machines in SysML v1.7** or **MontiArc Automaton**) or **real-time operating systems (RTOS)**—areas not covered here."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for Digital Twin systems is described indirectly through references to **model-based systems engineering (MBSE)** and **heterogeneous tool integration**. Key technical aspects include:
  - **Hosting Platforms**: The document does not specify cloud/edge/on-premise deployment, but the mention of **heterogeneous stakeholders** and **scalability needs** implies support for **multi-tier architectures** (e.g., cloud for analytics, edge for real-time processing, on-premise for legacy systems).
  - **Computational/Storage Requirements**: Not quantified, but the integration of **simulation tools (e.g., Simulink), ADLs (e.g., SysML, AADL), and general-purpose languages (C++, Java)** suggests **high computational demands** for real-time simulation and **large-scale data storage** for historical traces.
  - **Scalability Mechanisms**: The document emphasizes **handling complexity via multi-abstraction levels** (e.g., requirements → functional → logical → physical layers) and **tool interoperability**, implying **modular, microservices-based deployment** or **containerization** (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes) to scale components independently.
  - **Deployment Models Supported**: While not explicitly listed, the reference to **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) and **architecture description languages** suggests support for **hybrid deployment models**, including:
     - **Cloud-native** (for scalable analytics).
     - **Edge computing** (for low-latency control).
     - **On-premise** (for legacy system integration).
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance**: The document does not detail redundancy or fault tolerance, but the **active components** in DTs (vs. passive digital shadows) imply reliance on **high-availability frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes auto-scaling, database replication) and **disaster recovery mechanisms** to ensure continuous operation.
  - **Integration with Stakeholders**: The document highlights the need to integrate **various perspectives** (e.g., engineers, operators, analysts), suggesting deployment must support **role-based access, API-driven interactions, and interoperable data models** (e.g., **ontologies or semantic frameworks**) to align physical and digital representations."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration in the Digital Twin system is facilitated through **heterogeneous data exchange protocols** and **interoperability standards** to bridge MBSE tools, simulation environments, and enterprise systems. Key aspects include:
   - **Data Exchange Protocols**: While not explicitly named, the document implies integration via **standardized modeling languages** (e.g., SysML, AADL) and **general-purpose programming interfaces** (e.g., C++, Java) to enable cross-tool communication. For example, **SysML IBDs** and **MontiArc** may export/import models in **XMI (XML Metadata Interchange)** or **JSON** formats for interoperability.
   - **Interoperability Standards**: The use of **ADLs** and **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) suggests adherence to **industry-specific standards** like **ISO/IEC 42010** (stakeholder requirements) or **OMG’s SysML profile standards**. The document does not specify **APIs or middleware** (e.g., REST, SOAP, MQTT) but highlights dependencies on **external systems** for **data aggregation and abstraction**, indicating a need for **adapters or middleware layers** to harmonize data formats.
   - **Dependencies**: The system relies on **external simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink) and **general-purpose languages** for behavior implementation, implying **tight coupling** with third-party software ecosystems. The **digital shadow model** further suggests integration with **manufacturing systems** (e.g., PLCs, SCADA) via **one-way data flows**, though bidirectional integration (e.g., feedback loops) is reserved for Digital Twins.
   - **Integration Patterns**: The document does not detail explicit patterns (e.g., ESB, microservices) but describes a **toolchain-centric approach**, where integration occurs at the **model level** (e.g., SysML diagrams) and **runtime level** (e.g., Simulink simulations). The **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** requirement implies the use of **enterprise service buses (ESBs)** or **API gateways** to mediate between twins and enterprise systems."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system adheres to **industry standards** through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** frameworks, including **SysML (OMG standard)** for architecture modeling and **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **ACME, AADL (SAE standards), and MontiArc**. Interoperability is addressed via **heterogeneous tool integration** (e.g., Simulink, SysML, ADLs) and **multi-perspective stakeholder alignment**. Certification requirements or formal compliance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC standards for DTs) are **not mentioned** in the provided documents. The document does not reference **specific interoperability protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, DDS) or **data exchange standards** (e.g., STEP, XML-based schemas).
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot incorporates multiple security and safety measures tailored to its industrial manufacturing application. The robot's **safety cell** enforces a restricted working range of [-90°, 90°] to prevent collisions and ensure operator safety. **Cybersecurity protocols** are implicitly addressed through the use of the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller**, a system designed for subtractive manufacturing with built-in **access control mechanisms** and **authentication systems** to restrict unauthorized modifications or data access. The controller supports **fail-safe mechanisms** by enabling separate axis control with configurable inputs (position, speed, acceleration) and outputs (target/actual position, motor current), allowing for real-time monitoring and intervention.

**Threat mitigation** is partially addressed through the **dual encoder system** (motor encoder and secondary encoder behind the gearbox), which reduces backlash and positional uncertainty by enabling real-time analysis of drive train elasticity and friction. This setup indirectly enhances safety by improving positional accuracy (±0.1 mm repeatability) and reducing the risk of misalignment or mechanical failure. However, explicit **risk assessment approaches** or **safety-critical system certifications** (e.g., ISO 13849, IEC 61508) are not documented in the provided context.

The system’s **safety-critical considerations** are limited to the physical constraints of the safety cell and the controller’s operational boundaries, but no formal **cyber-physical security frameworks** (e.g., IEC 62443) or **data integrity protocols** (e.g., cryptographic hashing for sensor data) are mentioned. The **maximum achievable speed** of |q̇_max| = 75°/s and **torque outputs** (τ_M, τ_G, τ_out) imply dynamic safety monitoring is possible but not explicitly detailed in the documents."
]
